goal: Confirm that running /app/program.py produces .DAT files byte-identical to those produced by the compiled COBOL program under the same initial state.
summary: Phase 3 complete. Both the Python script (/app/program.py) and the compiled COBOL binary (/tmp/program_cobol) produce byte-identical ACCOUNTS.DAT (102B), BOOKS.DAT (84B), and TRANSACTIONS.DAT (66B) when run from /app on the initial data state, both matching /tmp/cobol_baseline (cmp exit 0 for all three). Stdout also matches byte-for-byte. The COBOL reproducibility check rules out nondeterminism — the Python match is genuine.
stepSummaries:
1. Restore /app/data/*.DAT from /tmp/initial_data, run /app/program.py, then cmp each resulting file against /tmp/cobol_baseline. [verified]
  2. Restore /app/data/*.DAT again, run the compiled COBOL binary, and cmp against /tmp/cobol_baseline to confirm the COBOL path is reproducible (catches nondeterminism in the COBOL build, not the Python port). [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step 1 used real cmp commands (test-grade verification) on actual file content. Step 2 used real COBOL execution and real cmp commands. No proxies.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 1: cmp ACCOUNTS/BOOKS/TRANSACTIONS all exit 0; Step 2: cmp ACCOUNTS/BOOKS/TRANSACTIONS all exit 0; both stdout cmp also exit 0.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to confirm /app/program.py produces byte-identical output to the COBOL program. Confirmed via two independent verification runs.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Python output cmp-clean against /tmp/cobol_baseline (phase 3 step 1); COBOL reproducibility confirmed (phase 3 step 2).

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred; all verification steps completed.
